title: Arrondissement of Blois

infobox.value: (French: *arrondissement de Blois*, [[aʁɔ̃dismɑ̃ də blwa]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is an [arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_France "Arrondissements of France") (district) of France, located in the [Loir-et-Cher](/wiki/Loir-et-Cher "Loir-et-Cher") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France"), [Centre-Val de Loire](/wiki/Centre-Val_de_Loire "Centre-Val de Loire"). It has 93 [communes](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France").[[2]](#cite_note-insee2-2) Its population is 151,096 (2021), and its area is 1,950.2 km2 (753.0 sq mi).[[3]](#cite_note-insee-3)
["Populations de référence 2023"](https://www.insee.fr/fr/statistiques/8681011) (in French). National Institute of Statistics and Economic Studies. 18 December 2025.
["Comparateur de territoire, géographie au 01/01/2023"](https://www.insee.fr/fr/statistiques/1405599?geo=ARR-411+ARR-412+ARR-413). [INSEE](/wiki/INSEE "INSEE"). Retrieved 10 December 2024.

["Populations légales 2012: 41 Loir-et-Cher"](https://www.insee.fr/fr/statistiques/fichier/2119595/dep41.pdf) (PDF). [INSEE](/wiki/Institut_national_de_la_statistique_et_des_%C3%A9tudes_%C3%A9conomiques "Institut national de la statistique et des études économiques"). December 2014.

The **arrondissement of Blois** (French: *arrondissement de Blois*, [[aʁɔ̃dismɑ̃ də blwa]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is an [arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_France "Arrondissements of France") (district) of France, located in the [Loir-et-Cher](/wiki/Loir-et-Cher "Loir-et-Cher") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France"), [Centre-Val de Loire](/wiki/Centre-Val_de_Loire "Centre-Val de Loire"). It has 93 [communes](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France").[[2]](#cite_note-insee2-2) Its population is 151,096 (2021), and its area is 1,950.2 km2 (753.0 sq mi).[[3]](#cite_note-insee-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissement_of_Blois&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The arrondissement of Blois was created in 1800.[[4]](#cite_note-splaf-4) In 2007 it lost the canton of Saint-Aignan to the [arrondissement of Romorantin-Lanthenay](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Romorantin-Lanthenay "Arrondissement of Romorantin-Lanthenay").[[4]](#cite_note-splaf-4) At the January 2017 reorganisation of the arrondissements of Loir-et-Cher, it gained three communes from the [arrondissement of Romorantin-Lanthenay](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Romorantin-Lanthenay "Arrondissement of Romorantin-Lanthenay") and four communes from the [arrondissement of Vendôme](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Vend%C3%B4me "Arrondissement of Vendôme"), and it lost 17 communes to the arrondissement of Romorantin-Lanthenay and two communes to the arrondissement of Vendôme.[[5]](#cite_note-arrete-5) In January 2019 it lost the commune [Courmemin](/wiki/Courmemin "Courmemin") to the arrondissement of Romorantin-Lanthenay.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

As a result of the reorganisation of the [cantons of France](/wiki/Cantons_of_France "Cantons of France") which came into effect in 2015, the borders of the cantons are no longer related to the borders of the arrondissements. The cantons of the arrondissement of Blois were, as of January 2015:[[7]](#cite_note-insee2012-7)

1. [Blois-1](/wiki/Canton_of_Blois-1 "Canton of Blois-1")
2. [Blois-2](/wiki/Canton_of_Blois-2 "Canton of Blois-2")
3. [Blois-3](/wiki/Canton_of_Blois-3 "Canton of Blois-3")
4. Blois-4
5. Blois-5
6. Bracieux
7. Contres
8. Herbault
9. Marchenoir
10. Mer
11. [Montrichard](/wiki/Canton_of_Montrichard "Canton of Montrichard")
12. Ouzouer-le-Marché
13. [Vineuil](/wiki/Canton_of_Vineuil "Canton of Vineuil")
